title: Repository Repair, Release, and Collaboration Capstone · History Brief: Minimal Repair Plan
summary: Recover and ship a repository containing mixed work, a conflict, a mistaken commit, a remote update, and a release deadline. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git minimal repair plan increment in a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.
Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Minimal Repair Plan
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v6.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Minimal Repair Plan contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.

content.text: A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v6.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Minimal Repair Plan condition decided your prediction.

content.value: githooks Manual — Controls client and server hook invocation, environment, parameters, bypass, and exit behavior.

steps.title: Bound Minimal Repair Plan
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Minimal Repair Plan application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.

content.text: Order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v6.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree. A defensible solution applies Minimal Repair Plan, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 4
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The shortest command sequence is the safest recovery plan. This fails the Minimal Repair Plan contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: githooks Manual (current as of 2026-07) — Controls client and server hook invocation, environment, parameters, bypass, and exit behavior.

steps.title: Build Minimal Repair Plan evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-minimal-repair-plan-oi8u8b" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Minimal Repair Plan into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-minimal-repair-plan-oi8u8b
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Repair the Minimal Repair Plan defect
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v5.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

A teammate acts on this belief: “The shortest command sequence is the safest recovery plan.” Which repair addresses the cause?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.

steps.title: Defend the Minimal Repair Plan model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Minimal Repair Plan: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.
